([American spelling](/wiki/American_English "American English")) or **defence** ([Commonwealth spelling](/wiki/English_in_the_Commonwealth_of_Nations "English in the Commonwealth of Nations")) is the action of preventing an opponent from scoring. The term may also refer to the tactics involved in defense, or a sub-team whose primary responsibility is defense. Similarly, a **defense player** or **defender** is a player who is generally charged with preventing the other team's forwards from being able to bear down directly on their own team's [goalkeeper](/wiki/Goalkeeper "Goalkeeper") or [goaltender](/wiki/Goaltender "Goaltender"). Such positions exist in [association football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football"), [ice hockey](/wiki/Ice_hockey "Ice hockey"), [water polo](/wiki/Water_polo "Water polo") and many other sports.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

(Redirected from [Defense (sport)](/w/index.php?title=Defense_(sport)&redirect=no "Defense (sport)"))

Preventing an opponent from scoring

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [icon](/wiki/File:Question_book-new.svg) | This article **needs additional citations for [verification](/wiki/Wikipedia:Verifiability "Wikipedia:Verifiability")**. Please help [improve this article](/wiki/Special:EditPage/Defense_(sports) "Special:EditPage/Defense (sports)") by [adding citations to reliable sources](/wiki/Help:Referencing_for_beginners "Help:Referencing for beginners"). Unsourced material may be challenged and removed. *Find sources:* ["Defense" sports](https://www.google.com/search?as_eq=wikipedia&q=%22Defense%22+sports) – [news](https://www.google.com/search?tbm=nws&q=%22Defense%22+sports+-wikipedia&tbs=ar:1) **·** [newspapers](https://www.google.com/search?&q=%22Defense%22+sports&tbs=bkt:s&tbm=bks) **·** [books](https://www.google.com/search?tbs=bks:1&q=%22Defense%22+sports+-wikipedia) **·** [scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=%22Defense%22+sports) **·** [JSTOR](https://www.jstor.org/action/doBasicSearch?Query=%22Defense%22+sports&acc=on&wc=on) *(January 2021)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

In many [team sports](/wiki/Team_sports "Team sports"), **defense** ([American spelling](/wiki/American_English "American English")) or **defence** ([Commonwealth spelling](/wiki/English_in_the_Commonwealth_of_Nations "English in the Commonwealth of Nations")) is the action of preventing an opponent from scoring. The term may also refer to the tactics involved in defense, or a sub-team whose primary responsibility is defense. Similarly, a **defense player** or **defender** is a player who is generally charged with preventing the other team's forwards from being able to bear down directly on their own team's [goalkeeper](/wiki/Goalkeeper "Goalkeeper") or [goaltender](/wiki/Goaltender "Goaltender"). Such positions exist in [association football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football"), [ice hockey](/wiki/Ice_hockey "Ice hockey"), [water polo](/wiki/Water_polo "Water polo") and many other sports.

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/2/2a/Carter_vs_Gasol%2C_Lakers_vs_Magic.jpg/250px-Carter_vs_Gasol%2C_Lakers_vs_Magic.jpg)](/wiki/File:Carter_vs_Gasol,_Lakers_vs_Magic.jpg)

[Pau Gasol](/wiki/Pau_Gasol "Pau Gasol") (yellow, outstretched arms) plays defense in a [basketball](/wiki/Basketball "Basketball") game by protecting the basket as his opponent [Vince Carter](/wiki/Vince_Carter "Vince Carter") (blue, holding ball) attempts to score with a [layup](/wiki/Layup "Layup")

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/3/39/Pittsburgh_Penguins%2C_Washington_Capitals%2C_Bryan_Rust_%2833744033514%29.jpg/250px-Pittsburgh_Penguins%2C_Washington_Capitals%2C_Bryan_Rust_%2833744033514%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:Pittsburgh_Penguins,_Washington_Capitals,_Bryan_Rust_(33744033514).jpg)

[Goaltender](/wiki/Goaltender "Goaltender") [Braden Holtby](/wiki/Braden_Holtby "Braden Holtby") (kneeling) defends the net from an incoming [shot on goal](/wiki/Shot_on_goal_(ice_hockey) "Shot on goal (ice hockey)") in an [ice hockey](/wiki/Ice_hockey "Ice hockey") game, while his teammate [Matt Niskanen](/wiki/Matt_Niskanen "Matt Niskanen") (#2) defends against [Sidney Crosby](/wiki/Sidney_Crosby "Sidney Crosby") from collecting the puck on a [rebound](/wiki/Rebound_(sports) "Rebound (sports)").

## Field sports

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Defense_(sports)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Field sports")]

### American football

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Defense_(sports)&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: American football")]

Main article: [Defense (American football)](/wiki/Defense_(American_football) "Defense (American football)")

### Association football

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Defense_(sports)&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Association football")]

Main article: [Defender (association football)](/wiki/Defender_(association_football) "Defender (association football)")

### Australian rules football

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Defense_(sports)&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Australian rules football")]

Main article: [Defender (Australian rules football)](/wiki/Defender_(Australian_rules_football) "Defender (Australian rules football)")

## Bat-and-ball sports

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Defense_(sports)&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Bat-and-ball sports")]

In [bat-and-ball sports](/wiki/Bat-and-ball_sports "Bat-and-ball sports"), the defending team is in the field, while the offensive team sends only a few players into the field to try to score at a time. These sports generally involve a member of the defense throwing the ball to a member of the offensive team, who then tries to hit it and run to various safe areas of the field to score points. Failing to hit the ball properly, or failing to reach the safe areas before the ball is used against the offensive player, can result in that player being left unable to score.

### Baseball

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Defense_(sports)&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Baseball")]

[Baseball](/wiki/Baseball "Baseball") is unlike most other competitive sports in that the defense is given control of the ball. Additionally, the number of players on the field at any given time is [lopsided](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/lopsided "wikt:lopsided") in favor of the defense which always has nine players on the field; the offense has between one and four.

Historically, each player on the team had a role both on offense and defense. Most amateur and professional leagues have adopted the [designated hitter](/wiki/Designated_hitter "Designated hitter") (DH) rule introduced by the [American League](/wiki/American_League "American League") of [Major League Baseball](/wiki/Major_League_Baseball "Major League Baseball"). In leagues that use the DH, pitchers usually do not bat, and the DH bats in lieu of the pitcher and does not play a defensive position. The main league that has not adopted the DH rule is the [Central League](/wiki/Central_League "Central League") of [Nippon Professional Baseball](/wiki/Nippon_Professional_Baseball "Nippon Professional Baseball").

Each play starts with the ball in the hands of the [pitcher](/wiki/Pitcher "Pitcher"), whose job as a member of the defense is to use his skills to somehow prevent the [batter](/wiki/Batter_(baseball) "Batter (baseball)") from reaching base. The pitcher throws the ball toward the [catcher](/wiki/Catcher "Catcher"), who must catch the pitched ball if it is not hit by the batter. In each half-[inning](/wiki/Inning "Inning"), the defense attempts to force three [outs](/wiki/Out_(baseball) "Out (baseball)").

There are three basic ways in which an out can occur:

* If three [strikes](/wiki/Strike_(baseball) "Strike (baseball)") are recorded against the batter,
* if a ball hit by a batter is caught by a defensive player before it hits the ground,
* if a [runner](/wiki/Baserunning "Baserunning") who is between bases or has not reached a base to which he is [forced](/wiki/Force_(baseball) "Force (baseball)") or [put out](/wiki/Putout "Putout") by a defensive player in possession of the ball.

If the batter manages to hit the ball, all nine defensive players become active and use the ball in attempting to prevent the batter from reaching base and [runners already on base](/wiki/Baserunning "Baserunning") from advancing or scoring. while the offense is busy attempting to move runners around the [baseball diamond](/wiki/Baseball_diamond "Baseball diamond") toward [home plate](/wiki/Home_plate "Home plate"), the defense uses the ball in various ways to achieve outs.

If the defense forces three outs, their team is moved into the offensive role. The exception is if it is the ninth or an [extra](/wiki/Extra_innings "Extra innings") inning and they are ahead, in which case, the game ends and the defensive team wins.

### Cricket

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Defense_(sports)&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Cricket")]

In [cricket](/wiki/Cricket "Cricket"), the [fielding team](/wiki/Fielding_(cricket) "Fielding (cricket)") is the defense, while the batting team is the offense; the batting team can only put two players on the field at a time.[[1]](#cite_note-1) By getting the batting team's batsmen [out](/wiki/Out_(cricket) "Out (cricket)") before they can hit the ball to the [boundary](/wiki/Boundary_(cricket) "Boundary (cricket)") or run between the two [batsmen's grounds](/wiki/Batsmen%27s_grounds "Batsmen's grounds"), the fielding team can prevent the batting team from scoring points. Players can be gotten out if they hit the ball in the air and it is caught by a defensive player before touching the ground, or if the ball hits a batsman's [wicket](/wiki/Wicket "Wicket"), either when delivered by the [bowler](/wiki/Bowling_(cricket) "Bowling (cricket)") or when the batsman is not in his ground. By getting all but one of the batting team's players out, the fielding team [ends](/wiki/End_of_an_innings "End of an innings") their opponent's [scoring turn](/wiki/Innings "Innings"), and may begin its own scoring turn next (though it can choose not to, in the case of the [follow-on](/wiki/Follow-on "Follow-on")).

## Water sports

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Defense_(sports)&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Water sports")]

### Beach volleyball

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Defense_(sports)&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Beach volleyball")]

Main article: [Defender (beach volleyball)](/wiki/Defender_(beach_volleyball) "Defender (beach volleyball)")

### Water polo

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Defense_(sports)&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Water polo")]

In [water polo](/wiki/Water_polo "Water polo"), if an offensive player, such as the center forward, has the ball in front of the goal, and the defender cannot steal the ball, he may intentionally commit a foul. The forward then has a free throw but must pass the ball off to another offensive player, rather than making a shot at the goal. Defensive perimeter players may also intentionally cause a foul and then move toward the goal, away from their attacker, who must take a free throw. This technique, called 'sloughing', allows the defense an opportunity to double-team the center forward and possibly steal the inbound pass. The water polo referee may refrain from declaring a foul caused by a defensive player, if in his judgment this would give the advantage to the defending team. This is known as the Advantage Rule.

## Floor and court sports

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Defense_(sports)&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: Floor and court sports")]

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [[icon]](/wiki/File:Wiki_letter_w_cropped.svg) | **This section is empty.** You can help by [adding to it](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Defense_(sports)&action=edit&section=). *(February 2025)* |

## Winter sports

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Defense_(sports)&action=edit&section=12 "Edit section: Winter sports")]

### Ice skating team sports

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Defense_(sports)&action=edit&section=13 "Edit section: Ice skating team sports")]

There are four officially established ice skating team sports worldwide, excluding their variants. This group includes: [bandy](/wiki/Bandy "Bandy"), [ice hockey](/wiki/Ice_hockey "Ice hockey"), [ringette](/wiki/Ringette "Ringette"), and [rinkball](/wiki/Rink_bandy#Rinkball "Rink bandy").

#### Ice hockey

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Defense_(sports)&action=edit&section=14 "Edit section: Ice hockey")]

Main article: [Defenceman](/wiki/Defenceman "Defenceman")

In [ice hockey](/wiki/Ice_hockey "Ice hockey"), there are normally two designated defencemen on the ice. A defenseman can be an offensive player better known for their ability to glean goals or assists rather than for their strong defensive play. These players are referred to as *offensive defensemen.*[[2]](#cite_note-2) Alternatively, a defenseman can play a more defensive role and rarely show up on the score sheet but remain important for their defensive prowess; these players are known as *stay-at-home defense*. This role, however, is becoming increasingly relegated to the past, as ice hockey teams demand more active play from their defenders.[[3]](#cite_note-3) There are also defensemen who are good at both; known as *two-way defensemen*. Defense is a team game in hockey, and when the team is not in possession of the puck (also known as *puck coverage*), the aim is to effectively suppress the puck pressure coming from the opponents.

#### Bandy

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Defense_(sports)&action=edit&section=15 "Edit section: Bandy")]

In [bandy](/wiki/Bandy "Bandy"), the number of defenders on the ice at a given time depends on the format being played, either "big ice" bandy which has a type of field format, or [rink bandy](/wiki/Rink_bandy "Rink bandy").

#### Ringette

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Defense_(sports)&action=edit&section=16 "Edit section: Ringette")]

Main article: [Ringette](/wiki/Ringette "Ringette")

In [ringette](/wiki/Ringette "Ringette"), there are two designated defenders on the ice in any normal game situation (the team is not serving any penalties). However, due to ringette's **first-three-in** rule these situations can vary; those playing in the defensive zone are not always designated defenders.

#### Rinkball

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Defense_(sports)&action=edit&section=17 "Edit section: Rinkball")]

Main article: [Rink bandy § Rinkball](/wiki/Rink_bandy#Rinkball "Rink bandy")

In [rinkball](/wiki/Rink_bandy#Rinkball "Rink bandy"), the number of defenders on the ice at a given time depends on the format being played.

### Skateless winter team sports

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Defense_(sports)&action=edit&section=18 "Edit section: Skateless winter team sports")]

There are three known organized skateless winter team sports worldwide, that are not organized forms of [snowball fighting](/wiki/Snowball_fight "Snowball fight"). This group includes: [broomball](/wiki/Broomball "Broomball"), [Spongee](/wiki/Spongee "Spongee") (a.k.a. "sponge hockey"), and [Moscow broomball](/wiki/Moscow_broomball "Moscow broomball").

#### Broomball

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Defense_(sports)&action=edit&section=19 "Edit section: Broomball")]

In [broomball](/wiki/Broomball "Broomball"), the number of defenders on the ice at a given time depends on the format being played.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

#### Spongee

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Defense_(sports)&action=edit&section=20 "Edit section: Spongee")]

In [spongee](/wiki/Spongee "Spongee") (a.k.a. "sponge hockey"), the number of defenders on the ice at a given time depends on the format being played.

#### Moscow broomball

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Defense_(sports)&action=edit&section=21 "Edit section: Moscow broomball")]

In [Moscow broomball](/wiki/Moscow_broomball "Moscow broomball"), the number of defenders on the ice at a given time depends on the format being played.

## Tag sports

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Defense_(sports)&action=edit&section=22 "Edit section: Tag sports")]

### Atya patya

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Defense_(sports)&action=edit&section=23 "Edit section: Atya patya")]

In [atya patya](/wiki/Atya_patya "Atya patya"), the defenders aim to tag players on the offensive team in order to eliminate them and prevent them from scoring.[[5]](#cite_note-:02-5)

### Kabaddi

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Defense_(sports)&action=edit&section=24 "Edit section: Kabaddi")]

In [kabaddi](/wiki/Kabaddi "Kabaddi"), the defenders aim to avoid being touched by the opposing team's raider; if the defenders can tackle the raider or "dash" the raider out-of-bounds so that the raider cannot return to the other half of the field,[[6]](#cite_note-6) then the defenders score 1 point.[[7]](#cite_note-7)

### Kho kho

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Defense_(sports)&action=edit&section=25 "Edit section: Kho kho")]

In [kho kho](/wiki/Kho_kho "Kho kho"), the defenders aim to avoid being tagged. Defenders take to the field in batches of three, with tagged defenders eliminated from play, and a new batch entering the field once the entire previous batch has been dismissed.[[8]](#cite_note-:0-8)
